title: micrococca wightii
intro: Micrococca wightii, also known as Kutki, is a plant species used in Ayurvedic medicine, belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. The medicinal properties of Micrococca wightii are attributed to its bitter and hepatoprotective properties, making it useful in treating liver and gallbladder issues. The health benefits of Micrococca wightii include its ability to improve digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of jaundice, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Micrococca wightii is commonly prepared into herbal supplements, teas, and decoctions for medicinal use, with recommended dosages typically ranging from 300-600 milligrams per day.
